title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa, also known as woolly marigold, is a subspecies of the calendula plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa include wound healing, skin protection, and relief from digestive issues and menstrual cramps, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, salves, creams, and ointments, which can be used topically to promote skin health and soothe irritated tissues.
benefits: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa has many benefits, such as its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and fight infections, making it a valuable tool in the treatment of various health conditions, including acne, eczema, and minor burns. Its antimicrobial and antifungal properties also make it effective in treating fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm. Additionally, this plant has been shown to have anti-aging properties, reducing fine lines and wrinkles, and its antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, contributing to a healthier and more youthful appearance. By incorporating calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa into one's skincare routine, individuals can enjoy improved skin health, reduced stress, and a sense of overall well-being, leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce wound healing time, and protect against cell damage. Its anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and rosacea, while its antimicrobial properties combat infections and promote healthy tissue repair, ultimately improving the overall appearance and feel of the skin. The antioxidant properties of this plant also protect against oxidative stress, which can lead to chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease, thereby promoting overall health and well-being. By harnessing the therapeutic potential of Calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa, individuals can experience improved skin health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced overall quality of life.
constituents: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, and sesquiterpene lactones like artabotrydine and artabotrysine, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These medicinal constituents can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing, reducing the risk of infection and scarring. They can also help to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of conditions such as eczema, acne, and rosacea, improving the quality of life for individuals affected by these conditions. Furthermore, the antioxidants present in calendula suffruticosa subsp. tomentosa can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the signs of aging, promoting healthier and more youthful-looking skin.
